payload.title: Launch comms — draft HN/Reddit/Lobsters/X posts
payload.description:
Prepare launch comms. Don't fire — that's bri's call on timing. This ticket prepares the assets.

Deliverables (one file each under docs/launch/ or similar):
- HN post: Title under 80 chars, opening that leads with the eval headline, link to repo. Pre-empt the obvious 'why not just clippy' / 'why not rust-analyzer' questions in the first comment from bri.
- r/rust: more technical tone, shows code samples in the post, mentions the audit'd ruleset.
- Lobsters: short, link-only with one paragraph of context. Lobsters readers prefer brevity.
- X/Twitter thread: 5-7 posts. Hook → number → before/after → install → repo link.

Constraints:
- All copy should be defensible against 'show me' — link the eval directory in every channel.
- No marketing-speak. Trust's audience is technical; overclaiming will be punished.
- Pre-write the response to the 3 most likely critiques: (1) why not contribute lints to clippy upstream, (2) sample size N=15 per provider, (3) why named-args instead of using struct-args.

Blocked by RT-56 (README), RT-57 (public repo exists), RT-58 (install works), RT-60 (WHY is essay-grade). Don't fire until all four are done.
